name: Andratx, Sant Elm, and Port d'Andratx Loop
name_original: Torre de Cala en Basset — circular desde Andratx por Sant Elm — Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana
description: This difficult 17.8-mile mountain biking loop from Andratx offers challenging terrain and stunning coastal views of Dragonera Island.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Andratx, located in southwestern Mallorca, offers diverse landscapes at the foothills of the Serra de Tramuntana mountain range. The region features rugged terrain, rocky slopes, and green vegetation, providing panoramic views. Its spectacular coastline includes small coves and beaches, allowing for routes that combine mountain and sea vistas. The area is characterized by a mix of mountainous environments and Mediterranean vegetation, including citrus and almond groves. Best mountain bike trails around Andratx The most popular mountain bike trail is Andratx, Sant Elm, and Port d'Andratx Loop, an 17.8 miles (28.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging terrain and coastal views, including glimpses of Dragonera Island. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View of Es Camp de Mar – View of Port Andratx loop from Es Pantaleu, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.8 km) path. This route features varied terrain with both mountain and sea views. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Es Camp de Mar – View of Port Andratx loop from Talaia del Cap Andritxol, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through coastal areas with strategic viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Andratx is defined by rugged terrain, coastal paths, and the foothills of the Serra de Tramuntana. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Andratx are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Andratx's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Andratx?
A: Andratx offers a diverse network of over 35 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, allowing riders to explore the rugged terrain, coastal paths, and the foothills of the Serra de Tramuntana.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Andratx?
A: Andratx benefits from a Mediterranean climate that encourages outdoor activities year-round. While mountain biking is possible throughout the year, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Andratx?
A: Yes, Andratx has trails suitable for beginners. While many routes offer challenging terrain, there are at least 4 easy and 16 moderate trails available. These provide a great way to experience the region's beauty without overly technical demands. Look for routes rated S0-S1 on the Singletrack Scale for a smoother experience.

Q: What kind of terrain will I encounter on mountain bike trails around Andratx?
A: Mountain biking in Andratx features a varied landscape. You can expect rugged terrain, rocky slopes, and green vegetation in the Serra de Tramuntana foothills, often with stunning panoramic views. Coastal routes offer spectacular sea vistas, sometimes including glimpses of Dragonera Island. Trails may include technical sections with rock gardens, but also smoother paths through citrus, almond, and olive groves.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Andratx?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Andratx are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34336370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Es Camp de Mar – View of Port Andratx loop from Es Pantaleu&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 24.1-mile path featuring both mountain and sea views.

Q: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Andratx offer numerous points of interest. You can discover historic watchtowers like the Torre del Cap Andritxol, which provide strategic viewpoints. The ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Trapa monastery&lt;/a&gt; are a significant attraction, offering sea views over Sa Dragonera. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/465708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cap Andritxol, Camp de Mar and Peguera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/606157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sa Dragonera&lt;/a&gt;, providing breathtaking vistas of the coastline and the protected island.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Andratx?
A: The mountain biking experience in Andratx is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, which combine challenging mountain terrain with stunning coastal views, and the opportunity to explore the unique Mediterranean vegetation and historic sites.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike routes in Andratx?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Andratx offers 18 difficult routes. These trails often feature technical sections, significant elevation changes, and rugged terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009170309/andratx-sant-elm-and-port-d-andratx-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andratx, Sant Elm, and Port d'Andratx Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.8 miles long with over 780 meters of elevation gain and offers challenging terrain and coastal views.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Andratx typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance, elevation, and your riding pace. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Es Camp de Mar – View of Port Andratx loop from Talaia del Cap Andritxol&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.4 miles long, typically takes around 1 hour 51 minutes. More challenging routes can take 2.5 hours or more.

Q: Where can I find information about bike rentals or repair shops in Andratx?
A: While komoot focuses on trail information, for bike rentals and repair shops in Andratx, it's best to check local tourism resources. The official &lt;a href="https://visit-andratx.com/en/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Andratx website&lt;/a&gt; or local search engines can provide up-to-date listings for services in the area, including Port d'Andratx and Sant Elm.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs near the mountain bike trails in Andratx?
A: Yes, the towns and villages surrounding the trails, such as Andratx, Port d'Andratx, and Sant Elm, offer various cafes and pubs where you can refuel and relax after your ride. These establishments are typically found within or close to the main settlements, making them easily accessible from many trailheads.

Q: Is it possible to combine mountain biking with other activities in Andratx?
A: Absolutely. Andratx's diverse landscape makes it ideal for combining mountain biking with other outdoor pursuits. You can explore the natural beauty of Sa Dragonera Natural Park by boat, visit historic watchtowers, or enjoy the many hiking opportunities, such as those around La Trapa. The region's stunning coastline also offers opportunities for relaxation by the sea.
tips: Nice and small fortified possession located in the municipality of Andratx that was restored at the end of the 20th century. The area offers a beautiful panoramic view of the valley and the mountains that rise around it.

Puerto de Andratx is a coastal town located to the west of the island.
It is a natural port with small mountains and cliffs around it and a beautiful promenade where the Lonja building is located as well as good restaurants and shops.

Amazing views on the island of Sa Dragonera. Located in the west of Mallorca, in front of the tower of Arrabassada and Cala en Basset, the island is the sixth largest of the Balearic archipelago and is protected as a natural area.

The 'Torre Cala d'en Basset' once protected the islanders from piracy. Today the watchtower is a meeting place for those who want to enjoy a beautiful view, to have a picnic or to take pictures. Others come to leave their graffiti marks.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Torre de Cala en Basset mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride. It demands good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for sections where you may need to dismount and push your bike, aligning with STS S3 - S6 difficulty levels for technical abilities.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The approximately 28.7 km trail features a diverse mix of surfaces, including significant portions of path, road, street, and singletrack. You'll encounter asphalt, gravel, unpaved, and natural sections, with some parts being rocky and uneven. There are also some alpine sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this mountain bike trail is approximately 2 hours and 41 minutes (9688 seconds). However, this can vary based on your fitness level, technical skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Tower of Cala en Basset, offering magnificent vistas of the Mediterranean Sea and Dragonera Island. Other notable points include the Capella de Son Orlandis, the Port d'Andratx Promenade, and a dedicated View of Sa Dragonera.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Torre de Cala en Basset mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Andratx and passing through Sant Elm. You can typically find parking in or around these towns to begin your ride. Specific parking areas may vary, so it's advisable to check local parking options upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Tramuntana region is beautiful year-round, but for mountain biking, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this trail in the Serra de Tramuntana Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Paraje Natural de la Sierra de Tramuntana, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on established trails within the natural park. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is the Torre de Cala en Basset mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often allowed on trails in the Sierra de Tramuntana, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for wildlife. Consider the difficulty and length of the trail before bringing your dog, as some sections may be too demanding for pets.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike trail intersect with other known routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Ruta de Pedra en Sec, Fars de Ponent, Llogarets de Ponent, and Port Andratx - Andratx trails. You'll also encounter smaller local paths like camí de s'Ermita and camí de Cala Egos.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Andratx and Sant Elm, the towns at the start and along the route, offer various options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. You'll find places to refuel and relax before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or children?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a 'difficult' mountain bike ride with demanding terrain and significant elevation changes, this specific trail is not recommended for families with young children or beginner riders. It requires advanced skills and good physical fitness.
